mncCalc.Description: It is an analog of a standard calculator, with the ability to retrieve data from a drawing, adding a result to a drawing, and the ability to manually write a formula for calculating
mncCalc.mi1: Absolute value of the specified number
mncCalc.mi2: The angle whose cosine is equal to the specified number
mncCalc.mi3: The angle whose sine is equal to the specified number
mncCalc.mi4: Angle, the tangent of which is equal to the specified number
mncCalc.mi5: The smallest integer is greater than or equal to a given number
mncCalc.mi6: E, raised to the specified degree
mncCalc.mi7: The largest integer is less than or equal to a given number
mncCalc.mi8: The remainder of dividing the indicated number by another specified number
mncCalc.mi9: The logarithm of the specified number
mncCalc.mi10: Logarithm with base 10 of the specified number
mncCalc.mi11: The greater of the two numbers
mncCalc.mi12: The smaller of the two numbers
mncCalc.mi13: The specified number, raised to the specified degree
mncCalc.mi14: Rounds a value to the nearest integer or specified number of decimal places
mncCalc.mi15: The value that specifies the sign of a number
mncCalc.h10: A number of symbols after comma
mncCalc.h12: Measure the distance between two points
mncCalc.h13: Get the sum of lengths from entities
mncCalc.h38: Insert result as a single line text
mncCalc.h39: Insert result into existing single line text (with replacement)
mncCalc.h40: Insert result into a table cell
mncCalc.h51: Get the height of the 3D Solid
mncCalc.msg1: An error has occurred. Possible formula for calculation is incorrect
mncCalc.msg10: Select several single-line or multi-line texts:
mncCalc.msg22: x1 does not belong\nto interval x0-x2
mncCalc.msg24: Select a 3D Solid that has the "Height" property
mncCalc.msg25: Select multiple 3D Solids that have the "Height" property
mncCalc.err2: Selected text does not contain numeric values
